judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with very similar rigor (same commands, same commit hashes, same conclusions); they are comparable in substance, with Report 2 adding slightly more explicit merge-base/ancestry proofs and Report 1 adding a bit more actionable detail (force-push caveats, test coverage gap).

report_1.evidence: `main`. The branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer` (tip `ac9d505`), which was itself an in-progress dependency. That dependency has since landed... there is no reason to target the now-obsolete `feat/weighted-scorer` branch

report_2.evidence: `main`. Per project documentation's rule "base every PR on the trunk," and since `feat/weighted-scorer` — the branch this work actually depends on — has already been merged into `main`... there is no live intermediate branch left to stack on.

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric`... was created on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`'s tip, `ac9d505`... `main` moved forward on 2026-06-13 via PR #42, which squash-merged that exact `feat/weighted-scorer` content into a single commit... the branch's starting point (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is a **dead-end/stale branch** — its content is fully subsumed by `main`, but its commits (`W1`/`W2`/`W3`) have no shared commit identity with `main`'s squash commit.

report_2.evidence: That starting point (`feat/weighted-scorer`) is **dead** — it has already been squash-merged into `main`... `git branch --contains feat/weighted-scorer` does not list `main`, confirming the original three W-commits are not literally in `main`'s history; they were merged via squash under a different commit hash, in PR #42.

report_1.evidence: additionally including: `scoring/metrics.py`: the `overlap()` helper (from `W3`) `scoring/scorer.py`: the entire weighted-scoring rewrite... This weighted-scorer content is not pairwise-metric work. It's present only because `feat/pairwise-metric` branched from `feat/weighted-scorer` before that work was (squash-)merged into `main`, and the branch was never rebased afterward.

report_2.evidence: This reintroduces the entire `overlap()` function and the `WEIGHTS`-based scoring rewrite in `scorer.py` — i.e., all of `feat/weighted-scorer`'s W1–W3 content... The other three commits are present purely as an artifact of branching from `feat/weighted-scorer` before it was squash-merged into `main`.

report_1.evidence: Easiest safe path: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Verify afterward with `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` — it should match today's 5-line two-dot diff, and now `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` (three-dot) should match it too, confirming the branch is truly rebased and merge-base has moved to `main`'s tip.

report_2.evidence: Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto current `main`... Re-verify the diff is scoped correctly after the rebase: `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric`... should show only the ~5-line pairwise addition (`pairwise_preference` in `scoring/metrics.py`, the grader-prompt line)
